items.title: Pentagon Says Iran War Has Cost U.S. $37.5 Billion
items.desc: Testifying before the Senate Appropriations Committee, Hegseth said the latest estimate represents a sharp increase from the nearly $29 billion the Pentagon reported in mid-May.

items.title: France Summons Iranian Chargé d'Affaires After French Diplomats Targeted in Tehran
items.desc: Paris demanded an investigation and accountability after two French diplomats were detained and questioned in Tehran, while Iran accused the diplomats of unacceptable conduct.

items.title: AI Chip Rally Lifts Wall Street Despite Rising Oil Prices
items.desc: The benchmark S&P 500 rose 0.9%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 372 points, or 0.7%, with about an hour left in trading. The Nasdaq Composite outperformed, climbing 1.3%, led by technology shares.

items.title: Syria Begins Steps to Establish Consular Presence in Erbil
items.desc: An official Syrian delegation has traveled to the Kurdistan Region to prepare for the opening of Syria's consulate in Erbil, aiming to expand consular services for Syrian citizens living in the Region.

items.title: Erbil International Airport Pauses Operations Following Drone Attack
items.desc: Erbil International Airport has resumed flight operations after a precautionary suspension of nearly one hour prompted by the security situation.

items.title: Damascus Jewish quarter returns to the spotlight
items.desc: In the eastern part of Damascus' Old City, just a short walk from Bab Sharqi (the Eastern Gate), the Jewish Quarter remains defined by its narrow stone alleys and traditional Damascene houses with inner courtyards, preserving the neighborhood's architectural character despite the departure of most of its longtime residents. items.title: Baghdad dismantles two child trafficking networks
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq's Interior Ministry said on Tuesday that its counter-trafficking directorate had dismantled two criminal networks involved in trafficking and unlawfully exploiting children in Baghdad and other provinces. The operation led to the arrest of the networks' members and the rescue of the child victims, who were placed under legal protection and care. All the suspects have been

items.title: Meat and chicken prices rise in Al-Sulaymaniyah amid supply pressures
items.desc: Shafaq News- Al-Sulaymaniyah Red meat and locally produced chicken prices have risen sharply in Al-Sulaymaniyah in recent days, adding pressure on households already struggling with the Kurdistan Region's prolonged economic crisis and delayed public-sector salaries. Residents told Shafaq News that the increase has pushed basic food items beyond the reach of many families. Before the economic crisi

items.title: Iran claims strikes disabled radar systems in Kuwait
items.desc: Shafaq News- Tehran Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said on Tuesday that its forces had struck and disabled early warning and tactical radar systems in Kuwait as part of the second wave of "Operation Nasr 2." items.title: Military campaign against Iran costs Washington $37.5B
items.desc: Shafaq News- Washington US military campaign against Iran has cost $37.5 billion so far, nearly $8 billion more than the last publicly disclosed estimate, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth revealed on Tuesday. Speaking to US lawmakers, Hegseth said the figure also includes projected costs through September 30. He accused Russia and China are helping Iran, to varying degrees, in carrying out some of it

items.title: Canada's Carney faces tough choice on US trade - fight back or hold steady
items.desc: The Canadian leader must weigh whether to make more concessions or take a tough stance as difficult trade talks drag on.

items.title: 'It's frustrating' - Canadians react to new US tariffs
items.desc: President Donald Trump has imposed a 50% tariff on a wide range of Canadian goods in retaliation for what he called "unequal treatment" of US products.

items.title: Iran war has cost US $37.5bn so far, Hegseth says
items.desc: Some senators pushed back on Hegseth's funding request as he testified at a pivotal time in the war.

items.title: 'Software error' led to almost 400 non-citizens voting in New Jersey, governor says
items.desc: The department of motor vehicles wrongly registered 6,600 non-Americans to vote, despite their indicating they were not US citizens.

items.title: 'He launched me in the air' - Grandfather recounts terrifying Yellowstone Park bison attack
items.desc: The 65-year-old says the animal was not showing any aggression until it picked a target - "that target happened to be me."

items.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires his military chief after protests and names a new one
items.desc: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief.

items.title: Former Canadian finance minister on Trump's tariff plan and its impact
items.desc: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Chrystia Freeland, former Canadian Finance Minister, about the potential impact of President Trump's proposed tariffs.

items.title: Nicaragua's President Ortega says no more elections, extending his 20-year rule
items.desc: The announcement effectively canceled Nicaragua's next elections, originally scheduled for November 2027.

items.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
items.desc: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
items.desc: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

items.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill 12, including family of six, medics say
items.desc: Firas al-Masri, his wife and their four children died in one attack, which the Israeli military says targeted a Hamas operative.

items.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
items.desc: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

items.title: Why Iran is risking so much over the Strait of Hormuz
items.desc: None of the choices facing Iran’s leaders offer a clear path to victory, as the conflict with the US continues.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
items.desc: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
items.desc: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.
